A function to determine the status of a number as prime or composite can be constructed using modulus. * * *  
The function uses modulus to check divisibility, and if a number is divisible by any number other than 1 and itself, it is composite. * *  
The function returns "prime" or "composite" based on the result of the modulus operation. * * *  
A number is considered prime if it has no divisors other than 1 and itself. * *  
The function checks divisibility by numbers up to the square root of the input value. * *  
If the modulus operation yields a remainder of 0, the number is composite. * *  
The function ensures that the status is determined accurately using the modulus operator. * *  
The function is concise and idiomatic in Python, adhering to best practices for readability. * *
